perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, the following terms have the respective meanings set forth below: "Acquiror" shall have the meaning set forth in the preamble hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, the following terms have the respective meanings set forth below: "Acquiror" shall refer to any entity designated as such within individual exhibits.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding the definition of 'Acquiror.' Originally, 'Acquiror' is explicitly defined as Sucampo Pharma, LLC in the preamble. The new definition introduces an In-Text Contradiction by suggesting the term can be redefined or mean different entities within other parts of the document (exhibits), potentially conflicting with the clearly defined role in the strategic alliance agreement. This could create confusion and disputes over which entity is bound by specific obligations under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2.02 Obligations of Acquiror. (a) Commencement of the Offer. Subject to the terms and conditions herein, Acquiror agrees to commence the Offer on August 27, 2015 (the "Launch Date") to acquire for cash (i) all of the issued and outstanding shares of Common Stock at the Share Offer Price and (ii) all of the outstanding Stock Options at the price as set out in Schedule I. The Offer shall be open for acceptance from the time of commencement until a time that is not earlier than 3:30 p.m. (Tokyo time) on the 30t h Japan Business Day from and including the Launch Date (as adjusted pursuant to Section 2.02(f) below, the "Offer Period").
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an ambiguity in the definition of 'Acquiror' by stating it can refer to any entity designated in exhibits. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (Section 2.02), which assumes a singular, clearly defined 'Acquiror' with specific obligations (e.g., commencing the offer on August 27, 2015). The change creates a strong contradiction because the obligation in Section 2.02 cannot be fulfilled if the identity of the Acquiror is uncertain or varies across exhibits, creating enforceability issues and meeting the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: For the purpose of creating a combined company that can drive considerable growth in global markets, including Japan, the Parties agree to form a strategic business alliance (the "Strategic Business Alliance") among Acquiror and SPI and its Affiliates (collectively, the "Sucampo Group") and the Company, subject to the successful Closing.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purpose of potentially exploring possible collaborations that may or may not lead to combined growth opportunities, the Parties agree to consider a strategic business relationship (the "Strategic Business Alliance") between Acquiror, SPI, and the Company, contingent upon ongoing assessment and mutual agreement. This alliance is subject to continuous review and may be terminated at any time by any party with 30 days written notice. The original intent was to create a strategic business alliance (the "Strategic Business Alliance") among Acquiror and SPI and its Affiliates (collectively, the "Sucampo Group") and the Company, subject to the successful Closing.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition explicitly states the purpose of creating a combined company. This perturbation introduces ambiguity by changing the nature of the alliance from a definitive agreement to 'form a strategic business alliance' to a tentative 'consider a strategic business relationship', subject to ongoing assessment and termination. Now it says the original intent *was* to create an strategic business alliance, this clearly creates an in-text contradiction within the section. This introduces significant uncertainty and contradicts later sections that assume a firm commitment to the strategic alliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2.01 Strategic Alliance. For the purpose of potentially exploring possible collaborations that may or may not lead to combined growth opportunities, the Parties agree to consider a strategic business relationship (the "Strategic Business Alliance") between Acquiror, SPI, and the Company, contingent upon ongoing assessment and mutual agreement. This alliance is subject to continuous review and may be terminated at any time by any party with 30 days written notice. The original intent was to create a strategic business alliance (the "Strategic Business Alliance") among Acquiror and SPI and its Affiliates (collectively, the "Sucampo Group") and the Company, subject to the successful Closing. The Parties intend to achieve the purpose of such Strategic Business Alliance by mutual cooperation in, among others, the following areas: (a) ensuring that the transaction contemplated in this Agreement would provide Sucampo Group with increased revenues—primarily from combining Sucampo Group's existing sales with those from the Company—enhanced profitability, strong cash flow generation and a robust balance sheet and the improved financial strength of SPI as the parent company would also accrue to the benefit of its subsidiaries, which will include the Company after the Closing; (b) ensuring that Sucampo Group and the Company together would have a deeper and broader development pipeline of potential drug candidates in development across four major therapeutic areas—gastroenterology, ophthalmology, autoimmune, and inflammation—and greater resources, both financially and operationally, to maximize these opportunities, and consistent with the Target Company's business strategy, such development pipelines (some of these drug candidates) could be out-licensed to external firms to create even greater value; and (c) ensuring that both Sucampo Group and the Company would have greater opportunity to conduct business development transactions, and through the relationships of the Company and increased presence in the Japanese market, Sucampo Group would gain greater access to the Japanese biotech community and Japan's well-regarded scientific institutions and researchers, and the Company would receive access to Sucampo Group's expertise in identifying, negotiating and managing key alliances.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a fundamental contradiction by shifting the nature of the 'Strategic Business Alliance' from a definitive agreement aimed at 'creating a combined company' to a tentative 'strategic business relationship' subject to 'ongoing assessment and mutual agreement' and potential termination. The changed text now implies that the 'original intent *was*' to create a strategic business alliance. The 'Contradicted Text' operates under the assumption of a firm alliance to 'achieve the purpose of such Strategic Business Alliance', outlining specific objectives contingent upon its existence. The change directly undermines the basis for these objectives, creating an in-text contradiction. This ambiguity regarding the fundamental nature and stability of the alliance creates a significant ambiguity and direct contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' because the nature of the alliance is defined inconsistently.

perturbation.original_text: The Indemnifying Party's liability for all claims made under this Agreement shall be subject to the following limitations: (i) the Indemnifying Party shall […***…] for such claims until the […***…] of the […***…] shall […***…] of the […***…] by the […***…] of all of the […***…] and […***…] of […***…], in which case the Indemnifying Party shall be liable only for the […***…] of the [… ***…] of the […***…] by the […***…] of all of the […***…] and […***…] of […***…], and (ii) the Indemnifying Party's […***…] for [… ***…] shall not […***…] of the […***…] by the […***…] of all of the […***…] and […***…] of […***…].
perturbation.changed_text: The Indemnifying Party's liability for all claims made under this Agreement shall be unlimited and not subject to any limitations. The Indemnifying Party's liability for all claims made under this Agreement shall be subject to the following limitations: (i) the Indemnifying Party shall […***…] for such claims until the […***…] of the […***…] shall […***…] of the […***…] by the […***…] of all of the […***…] and […***…] of […***…], in which case the Indemnifying Party shall be liable only for the […***…] of the [… ***…] of the […***…] by the […***…] of all of the […***…] and […***…] of […***…], and (ii) the Indemnifying Party's […***…] for [… ***…] shall not […***…] of the […***…] by the […***…] of all of the […***…] and […***…] of […***…].
perturbation.explanation: This creates a direct contradiction by stating the Indemnifying Party's liability is both unlimited and subject to limitations. Initially, 'The Indemnifying Party's liability for all claims made under this Agreement shall be unlimited and not subject to any limitations', immediately creating an in-text contradiction with the subsequent limitations outlined in (i) and (ii).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 5.04 Limitations. The Indemnifying Party's liability for all claims made under this Agreement shall be unlimited and not subject to any limitations. The Indemnifying Party's liability for all claims made under this Agreement shall be subject to the following limitations: (i) the Indemnifying Party shall […***…] for such claims until the […***…] of the […***…] shall […***…] of the […***…] by the […***…] of all of the […***…] and […***…] of […***…], in which case the Indemnifying Party shall be liable only for the […***…] of the [… ***…] of the […***…] by the […***…] of all of the […***…] and […***…] of […***…], and (ii) the Indemnifying Party's […***…] for [… ***…] shall not […***…] of the […***…] by the […***…] of all of the […***…] and […***…] of […***…]. Notwithstanding the above provisions of this Section 5.04, the limitations provided in this Section 5.04 shall not apply to (i) any claim for fraud or intentional misrepresentation or (ii) any claim for breach of any agreement or covenant contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself by stating the Indemnifying Party's liability is both 'unlimited and not subject to any limitations' and then immediately stating it is subject to limitations. This creates a clear and impactful in-text contradiction as it presents two opposing conditions for liability within the same section, leading to potential confusion and disputes about which condition applies.
